docs.title: Welcome to Stride
docs.text:
Sprint-Powered, Spec-Driven Development for AI Agents

Stride is a powerful sprint management framework designed for developers working with AI coding agents. It brings structure, visibility, and control to AI-assisted development through a status-driven workflow.

docs.title: What is Stride?
docs.text: Stride transforms chaotic AI-assisted coding into organized, trackable sprints. Instead of losing context across multiple AI conversations, you maintain a clear history of what was built, why, and how.

docs.title: Key Benefits
docs.text:
• 🎯 Organized Development - Status-based folder system (proposed → active → blocked → review → completed)
  • 🤖 Multi-Agent Support - Works with 20+ AI coding tools (Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, etc.)
  • 📊 Complete Visibility - Track every change, monitor progress in real-time
  • ✅ Quality Control - Built-in validation and health checks
  • 📈 Rich Reporting - Export to JSON, Markdown, CSV, or HTML

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
# Initialize Stride
stride init
stride agent init

# Create a sprint
stride create --title "Add user authentication"
# → SPRINT-7K9P created

# Work on it
stride move SPRINT-7K9P active
stride watch SPRINT-7K9P  # Monitor in real-time

# Complete it
stride validate SPRINT-7K9P
stride move SPRINT-7K9P completed

docs.title: The Problem
docs.text:
When using AI coding agents like Claude Code or Cursor, developers face:

  • Lost context across multiple AI conversations
  • No clear history of what was implemented
  • Difficulty tracking changes and decisions
  • Lack of quality validation
  • No coordination between different AI tools

docs.title: The Solution
docs.text:
Stride provides:

  • Sprint-Based Workflow - Every feature becomes a trackable sprint
  • Status Management - Visual folder-based lifecycle
  • AI Agent Integration - Unified commands across 20+ tools
  • Quality Gates - Validation before completion
  • Comprehensive Reporting - Export and share progress

docs.title: Core Workflow
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[Create Sprint] --> B[Move to Active]
    B --> C[Implement with AI]
    C --> D[Validate Quality]
    D --> E[Move to Review]
    E --> F[Complete Sprint]
    F --> G[Archive & Report]

    style A fill:#ffebee
    style B fill:#e3f2fd
    style C fill:#e8f5e9
    style D fill:#fff9c4
    style E fill:#f3e5f5
    style F fill:#c8e6c9
    style G fill:#b2dfdb

docs.title: Getting Started with Stride
docs.text: Welcome! This guide will help you install Stride and create your first sprint in less than 10 minutes.

docs.title: What You'll Learn
docs.text:
By the end of this guide, you'll:

  • ✅ Install Stride on your system
  • ✅ Initialize Stride in a project
  • ✅ Configure AI agent integrations
  • ✅ Create and manage your first sprint
  • ✅ Understand the core workflow

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before you begin, ensure you have:

  • Python 3.11+ installed
  • Git for version control
  • Terminal/Command Line access
  • (Optional) An AI coding agent like Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf

docs.title: Next Steps

    Install Stride and set up your development environment.

docs.title: Solo Developer
docs.text:
You're building a side project with AI assistance and want to track what gets implemented.

Stride helps you: - Track implementation history - Validate code quality - Export progress reports - Switch between AI tools seamlessly

docs.title: Team Development
docs.text:
Your team uses various AI agents and needs coordination.

Stride helps you: - Standardize workflows across agents - Track who implemented what - Generate team analytics - Maintain audit trails

docs.title: AI-First Workflow
docs.text:
You primarily code with AI assistants and need structure.

Stride helps you: - Organize AI conversations into sprints - Prevent context loss - Apply quality gates - Build reliable artifacts

docs.title: Time Investment
docs.text:
• Installation: 5 minutes
  • First Sprint: 10 minutes
  • Learning Curves: 30 minutes
  • Proficiency: 2-3 sprints

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: This guide covers installing Stride on various platforms.

docs.title: System Requirements
docs.text:
• Python: 3.11 or higher
  • Operating System: Windows, macOS, or Linux
  • Memory: 100MB+ free space
  • Network: Internet access for initial download

docs.title: Installation Methods
docs.text:
From Source (Recommended)From PyPI (Coming Soon)Using pipx (Isolated) 
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/saranmahadev/Stride.git
cd Stride

# Create virtual environment
python -m venv venv

# Activate virtual environment
# On Windows:
venv\Scripts\activate
# On macOS/Linux:
source venv/bin/activate

# Install dependencies
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Install Stride in development mode
pip install -e .

# Verify installation
stride --version

# Install from PyPI
pip install stride-ai

# Verify installation
stride --version

# Install pipx if not already installed
python -m pip install --user pipx
python -m pipx ensurepath

# Install Stride
pipx install stride-ai

# Verify installation
stride --version

docs.title: Windows
docs.text:
Windows Users

Use PowerShell or Windows Terminal for the best experience.

# Install Python from python.org if not installed
# Then follow the "From Source" instructions above

# Optional: Add to PATH
$env:PATH += ";$PWD\venv\Scripts"

docs.title: macOS
docs.text:
# Ensure Python 3.11+ is installed
python3 --version

# Install Homebrew if not installed
/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"

# Install Python 3.11+
brew install python@3.11

# Follow "From Source" instructions above

docs.title: Linux
docs.text:
# Ubuntu/Debian
sudo apt update
sudo apt install python3.11 python3.11-venv python3-pip

# Fedora/RHEL
sudo dnf install python3.11 python3-pip

# Follow "From Source" instructions above

docs.title: Verifying Installation
docs.text:
After installation, verify everything works:

# Check version
stride --version
# Output: Stride version 1.0.0

# Check help
stride --help
# Should show all available commands

# Run health check
stride doctor
# Should show "Installation: ✓ Stride is properly installed"

docs.title: For Development
docs.text:
pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install pytest pytest-cov black mypy flake8

docs.title: For Documentation
docs.text: pip install mkdocs mkdocs-material

docs.title: Python Version Issues
docs.text:
Python 3.11+ Required

Stride requires Python 3.11 or higher.

# Check your Python version
python --version

# If too old, install newer version
# Windows: Download from python.org
# macOS: brew install python@3.11
# Linux: Use your package manager

docs.title: Permission Errors
docs.text:
# If you get permission errors on Linux/macOS
sudo pip install -r requirements.txt

# Or use virtual environment (recommended)
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Import Errors
docs.text:
# If you get "Module not found" errors
pip install -r requirements.txt --force-reinstall

# Or reinstall Stride
pip install -e . --force-reinstall

docs.title: Command Not Found
docs.text:
# Ensure virtual environment is activated
source venv/bin/activate  # macOS/Linux
venv\Scripts\activate  # Windows

# Or add to PATH
export PATH="$PATH:$(pwd)/venv/bin"  # macOS/Linux
$env:PATH += ";$PWD\venv\Scripts"  # Windows PowerShell

docs.title: From Source
docs.text:
cd Stride
git pull origin main
pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install -e . --upgrade

docs.title: From PyPI (Future)
docs.text: pip install --upgrade stride-ai

docs.title: Uninstalling
docs.text:
# If installed with pip
pip uninstall stride-ai

# Remove virtual environment
rm -rf venv  # macOS/Linux
Remove-Item -Recurse venv  # Windows

# Remove cloned repository
cd ..
rm -rf Stride  # macOS/Linux
Remove-Item -Recurse Stride  # Windows

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Installation complete! Now:

  1. Quick Start Guide - Create your first sprint
  2. Core Concepts - Understand how Stride works
  3. User Guide - Explore all features

Having trouble? Check our FAQ or open an issue.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Get up and running with Stride in 5 minutes! This guide will walk you through creating your first sprint.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Make sure you've installed Stride first.

docs.title: Step 1: Initialize Your Project
docs.text:
Navigate to your project directory and initialize Stride:

cd your-project
stride init

You'll see output like:

✓ Created stride/ directory structure
✓ Created stride/sprints/ folders
✓ Created stride/specs/ folder
✓ Created stride/project.md
✓ Stride initialized successfully!

docs.title: Step 2: Configure AI Agents (Optional)
docs.text:
If you're using AI coding assistants like Claude or Cursor:

stride agent init

Select your AI tools:

? Select AI agents to configure:
  [x] Claude Code
  [x] Cursor  
  [ ] Windsurf
  [ ] GitHub Copilot

✓ Configured 2 agents
✓ Created CLAUDE.md
✓ Created CURSOR.md

docs.title: Step 3: Authenticate (Optional)
docs.text:
Track your work with user authentication:

stride login

Enter your details:

? Name: Your Name
? Email: you@example.com

✓ Authenticated as you@example.com

docs.title: Step 4: Create Your First Sprint
docs.text:
Create a sprint for a new feature:

stride create \
  --title "Add dark mode toggle" \
  --description "Implement a dark mode theme switcher" \
  --priority high

Output:

✓ Created sprint: SPRINT-A1B2
✓ Location: stride/sprints/proposed/SPRINT-A1B2/
✓ Files created:
  - proposal.md

Next steps:
  stride show SPRINT-A1B2           # View sprint details
  stride move SPRINT-A1B2 active    # Start working

docs.title: Step 5: View Sprint Details
docs.text:
Check what was created:

stride show SPRINT-A1B2

You'll see:

╭─ Sprint: SPRINT-A1B2 ──────────────────────╮
│                                             │
│ Title:    Add dark mode toggle             │
│ Status:   proposed                          │
│ Priority: high                              │
│ Created:  2025-11-22 10:30:00 UTC          │
│ Author:   you@example.com                   │
│                                             │
│ Description:                                │
│ Implement a dark mode theme switcher       │
│                                             │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────╯

Available files:
  📄 proposal.md

docs.title: Step 6: Move to Active
docs.text:
Start working on the sprint:

stride move SPRINT-A1B2 active

Output:

✓ Moved SPRINT-A1B2: proposed → active
✓ Location: stride/sprints/active/SPRINT-A1B2/

docs.title: Step 7: Monitor Progress
docs.text:
Watch your sprint in real-time:

stride watch SPRINT-A1B2

Or check status:

stride status SPRINT-A1B2
stride progress SPRINT-A1B2
stride timeline SPRINT-A1B2

docs.title: Step 8: Validate Quality
docs.text:
Before completing, validate your work:

stride validate SPRINT-A1B2

Output:

✓ Validating SPRINT-A1B2...

Structure:
  ✓ Sprint folder exists
  ✓ Required files present
  ✓ Metadata valid

Quality:
  ✓ Title is descriptive
  ✓ Description is clear
  ✓ Proper formatting

Overall Score: 95/100 (Excellent)

docs.title: Step 9: Complete the Sprint
docs.text:
Move to completed:

stride move SPRINT-A1B2 completed

Output:

✓ Moved SPRINT-A1B2: active → completed
✓ Location: stride/sprints/completed/SPRINT-A1B2/
✓ Sprint completed successfully!

docs.title: Bonus: Export Report
docs.text:
Generate a report of your work:

stride export --format markdown --output report.md

Output:

✓ Exported 1 sprint(s) to report.md

Summary:
  Total sprints: 1
  Completed: 1
  Format: markdown

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Congratulations! You've completed your first Stride sprint. 🎉

Now explore:

  • 📖 Core Concepts - Understand the workflow
  • 🤖 AI Agents - Integrate with AI tools
  • 📚 User Guide - Learn all commands
  • 🎓 Tutorials - Real-world examples

docs.title: Common Commands Cheat Sheet
docs.text:
# Sprint Management
stride create --title "Feature"     # Create sprint
stride list                          # List all sprints
stride show SPRINT-ID                # View details
stride move SPRINT-ID <status>       # Change status

# Monitoring
stride status SPRINT-ID              # Check status
stride progress SPRINT-ID            # View progress
stride watch SPRINT-ID               # Live monitor
stride timeline SPRINT-ID            # See history

# Quality & Reports
stride validate SPRINT-ID            # Check quality
stride doctor                        # Health check
stride export                        # Generate report

# Configuration
stride agent init                    # Setup AI agents
stride config get user.name          # Get config value
stride login                         # Authenticate

docs.title: Tips for Success
docs.text:
Best Practices

  • Create sprints for discrete features
  • Move through statuses methodically
  • Validate before completing
  • Export reports regularly
  • Use descriptive titles

Common Mistakes

  • Creating sprints that are too large
  • Skipping validation steps
  • Not using status folders properly
  • Forgetting to authenticate
